message:
fix(lab): use upper bits of LCG state for Fisher-Yates shuffle quality

The instrumented future's deterministic shuffle was using raw
rng_state modulo (i+1) for the Fisher-Yates swap index.  LCG
generators have notoriously short periods in their lower bits,
which degrades shuffle quality.  Shifting right by 32 before the
modulo uses the higher-quality upper bits instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
